gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/255)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/255)
text: مَنْ أَمَاطَ أَذًى عَنْ طَرِيقِ مَكَّةَ كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ حَسَنَةً وَ مَنْ كَتَبَ لَهُ حَسَنَةً لَمْ يُعَذِّبْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Yahya Bin Al Mubarak, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who removes a harmful obstruction from a stree of Makkah, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Write for him a good deed; and the one for whom a good deed is Written, He<sup>azwj</sup> would not Punish him’.
en.sarwar: 34. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Yahya’ ibn al-Mubarak from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ‘If one removes something causing hardships to people on the way to Makkah, Allah writes down for him one good deed and one for whom a good deed is written, he is not subjected to punishment.’”
